entries.definition:
q–r–ṭ/s paper, parchment; strong young she-camel; tall, fair young woman; to hit the mark. It has been suggested that قِرْطَاس qirṭās came to Arabic through Ethiopic and Syriac. Of this root, two forms occur once each in the Qur’an: قِرْطَاس qirṭās and قَرَاطِيس qarāṭīs.
قِرْطَاس qirṭās [n.; pl. قَرَاطِيس qarāṭīs] paper, parchment (6:7) وَلَوْ نَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكَ كِتَابًا فِي قِرْطَاسٍ فَلَمَسُوهُ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ لَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ هَٰذَا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ even if We had sent down to you actual writing inscribed on parchment, and they had touched it with their own hands, those who disbelieve would still say, ‘This is nothing but manifest sorcery.’
entries.definition_english:
q–r–ṭ/s paper, parchment; strong young she-camel; tall, fair young woman; to hit the mark. It has been suggested that قِرْطَاس qirṭās came to Arabic through Ethiopic and Syriac. Of this root, two forms occur once each in the Qur’an: قِرْطَاس qirṭās and قَرَاطِيس qarāṭīs.
قِرْطَاس qirṭās [n.; pl. قَرَاطِيس qarāṭīs] paper, parchment (6:7) وَلَوْ نَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكَ كِتَابًا فِي قِرْطَاسٍ فَلَمَسُوهُ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ لَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ هَٰذَا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ even if We had sent down to you actual writing inscribed on parchment, and they had touched it with their own hands, those who disbelieve would still say, ‘This is nothing but manifest sorcery.’
